title: NQ Probe Reversal Toward Upper Liquidity
focus_questions: Is this still only a proof trade, or has the reversal earned promotion?
Did the upper attractor remain active throughout the move?

summary.thesis: A small reversal probe becomes useful when downside continuation fails cleanly and price is still being pulled toward upper liquidity.
summary.operator_translation: The first entry stays small until the market proves acceptance upward.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when failed downside continuation, upper-liquidity attraction, and reclaim behavior appear together.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: failed downside continuation under an active upper attractor. Required evidence: non-continuation lower, attractor still active, and reclaim of a usable threshold. Do not trade: if this is only a blind bottom pick or the attractor is gone. Real trigger: a small proof reclaim that starts reducing distance to the upper target. Failure point: no upward acceptance after the probe.

when_relevant: Price tests lower but cannot continue cleanly.
An upper liquidity band still attracts price.
The reversal begins to reclaim a usable threshold.
required_evidence: downside continuation fails instead of accepting lower
a reclaim such as EMA20 or a local pivot appears after the probe
confirmation_signals: the upper attractor remains relevant while distance to it shrinks
price reclaims a usable threshold after the probe
invalidation_signals: price cannot reclaim any meaningful threshold after the probe
the upper attractor disappears or is no longer relevant
broader downside continuation quickly resumes with acceptance lower
no_trade_conditions: Do not bottom-pick purely because price is down; failed downside continuation must be visible first.
Do not size the first probe like a full reversal when the market has not yet shown upward acceptance.
Do not keep buying if the upper attractor disappears or the broader script still clearly favors downside continuation.

entry_archetypes.name: first proof reclaim
entry_archetypes.description: Small entry after failed downside continuation and the first usable reclaim.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the reclaim happens directly into stronger overhead resistance with no room.

entry_archetypes.name: pullback after reclaim
entry_archetypes.description: Add or re-enter on a controlled pullback after the market proves the first reclaim.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the pullback loses the reclaimed threshold immediately.

management_notes: Keep the first probe small until acceptance improves.
Track whether the move reaches the upper attractor or fails at the first reclaim threshold.
If the attractor is tagged, reassess whether the next script is harvest, rejection, or continuation.
review_questions: Was the probe actually taken after downside failure, or was it an emotional catch attempt?
What evidence upgraded the idea from a probe into a real reversal candidate?
Did the move reclaim a meaningful threshold, or only print a brief bounce before failing?
